# Project Charta
## Context and Scope

This project focuses on the socio-economic, geographic, and economic analysis of meteorite discoveries worldwide. While NASA records where meteorites were found, the data is heavily biased by human presence and environmental conditions.

The "Meteorite Treasure Map" aims to deconstruct this "Discovery Gap" by correlating NASA data with population density (Human Presence), Aridity (Preservation), and Land Cover (Visibility). Additionally, it integrates a market-value estimation engine based on meteorite classification and mass. The goal is to shift from a map of findings to an interactive "Opportunity & Value Map" that protects collectors from fraud and helps explorers find high-potential, unrecorded search zones.

## Project Objectives and Success Criteria
Objective:
Enable users to understand that meteorite distribution maps are often maps of human activity and geography. The project identifies "Discovery Gaps" by overlaying NASA data with socio-economic and environmental layers.

Success Criteria:
- Users can identify the correlation between population density and discovery rates.
- Users can calculate an Estimated Market Value (EMV) for specific meteorite classes.
- The visualization successfully highlights "High-Potential" zones where meteorites are likely hidden but unrecorded.

Quantitative criteria:
- At least 5 interactive visualizations (Bivariate Map, Price-Scatterplot, etc.).
- Integration of 4 distinct data pillars (NASA, World Bank, Aridity Index, Market Price Matrix).
- Data includes more than 30,000 observations.

Out of Scope:
- No real-time tracking of new meteor showers.
- No advanced machine learning (rule-based statistical scoring only).

## Stakeholder Analysis
Stakeholders include:

- Project Team: Responsible for data synthesis and dashboard architecture.
- Lecturers: Evaluating the analytical depth and handling of data bias.
- Meteorite Collectors & Investors: Requiring independent price benchmarks to avoid scams.
- Expedition Planners: Seeking high-probability, under-searched areas for field research.

The project team develops the product, lecturers assess its quality, and users benefit from understanding the data.

## User Analysis

PERSONA 1
Name: Lukas (University Student)
Age: 23
Quote: "I need hard data to know what my find is actually worth on the market."

![ ](pics/lukas.png){ width=70% }

Background & Behavior:
Lucas is a dedicated student who finances his studies through an unusual but lucrative hobby: meteorite hunting. He has read deeply into the subject, knows the technical terminology, and has already found and sold genuine pieces online. He is highly digitally literate and frequently uses collector forums and auction platforms.

Goals:

- To get the maximum yet fair price for his finds to pay for his tuition and equipment.

- To quickly and reliably determine how rare a specific meteorite class truly is based on its mass.

Pain Points:

- The meteorite market lacks transparency. Lucas is often unsure whether he is pricing his finds too low or getting ripped off by commercial buyers.

- He lacks a fast, data-driven tool to help classify rarity without having to spend hours digging through old catalogs.

Key Features Used on Your Site:
The interactive Class Calculator. He uses this tool as a quick benchmark method. He selects the visual description of his find, enters the mass, and immediately sees its statistical rarity. This helps him justify his pricing on eBay or in collector communities.

PERSONA 2
Name: Sarah (Fit Retiree)
Age: 67
Quote: "My camper, the vastness of the desert, and the chance to find a piece of the universe—that is my perfect vacation."

![ ](pics/sarah.png){ width=70% }

Background & Behavior:
Sarah is a highly active retiree who loves to travel. Her life changed when she accidentally found a small meteorite during a hike. Since then, she has become a passionate amateur collector. She strategically plans her holiday routes (mostly in her camper van) around areas where she has the best chances of making a new discovery.

Goals:

- To have a meaningful, exciting mission for her long holiday road trips.

- To explore large, undiscovered areas that offer a high probability of yielding a genuine find.

Pain Points:

- She does not have a background in science. Raw NASA databases, endless Excel spreadsheets, or complex GIS software are far too complicated and overwhelming for her.

- She needs a highly accessible, extremely user-friendly environment that instantly shows her where driving her camper will be worth the effort.

Key Features Used on Your Site:
The Storytelling and the Interactive Treasure Map (Map C). The clean, scrollable design caters perfectly to her needs. She uses the map to cross-reference the "Discovery Gap" (the golden points) with her upcoming travel route. If a region in southern Spain or North Africa lights up, she plans her next major stop exactly there.





## Situation Assessment
Resources:
- Dataset containing meteorite landings with attributes such as mass, location, class, and year.
- Scientific Data: NASA Meteorite Landings.
- Socio-Economic Data: World Bank (GDP per capita, Population Density).
- Environmental Data: MODIS Land Cover and Aridity Index (to calculate visibility and preservation).
- Team members with basic Python and data visualization knowledge.
- Tools: Python, Quarto, GitHub.

Constraints:
- Limited time until project deadlines.
- Requirement for reproducibility.

Risks:
- Spatial Joining: Technical challenge of merging point-data (NASA) with raster-data (Climate/Population).
- Price Volatility: Market prices fluctuate; mitigated by using 5-year average benchmarks.

## Visualization Concept
The visualization product is designed to translate the project objectives into an intuitive and accessible representation of meteorite data. It enables users to explore patterns in meteorite distribution, mass, and classification in a structured and visually clear way, addressing their need for simple and understandable insights.

Product form:
The chosen format is a data story presented as a web-based application using Quarto. This format allows a guided narrative, where users are introduced step by step to the most important aspects of the dataset. At the same time, it allows light exploration, combining storytelling with elements of an exploratory tool. It follows a narrative: from the "Illusion" (NASA's raw map) to the "Reality" (Adjusted for human and geographic bias).

Narrative Structure: The story follows a path from The Illusion (NASA's raw map) to The Explanation (Overlaying human/environmental filters) to The Revelation (The Opportunity Map and the financial worth of undiscovered falls).

Visual encodings:

- Bivariate Choropleth Map: Intersection of Population Density vs. Discovery Frequency.
- Valuation Scatterplot: Correlation of Meteorite Class vs. Market Value.
- The "Fair Value" Tooltip: Interactive pop-up for each NASA point, displaying an estimated price based on mass and rarity.
- Heatmap of the "Discovery Gap": Highlighting arid, uninhabited areas with high preservation potential.

These visual encodings are chosen to represent key analytical dimensions such as distribution, comparison, location, and temporal trends.

Interactivity:

- Layer Toggles: Switch between "Raw Finds" and "Discovery Probability."
- Region Inspector: Click a region to see its "Discovery Efficiency Score" (based on Aridity and Population).
- Valuation Input: Users can input custom mass/class to test "What-if" scenarios for potential finds

Narrative and annotation:
The visualization follows a clear storytelling structure: introduction, exploration of key variables, and summary of findings. Each visualization is accompanied by short explanatory text and annotations to guide the user and highlight key insights. This reduces cognitive load and helps users interpret the data correctly.

Target medium and integration:
The visualization product will be published as a web-based application using GitHub Pages. This ensures easy accessibility across devices such as laptops and tablets without requiring additional software installation.

Value of the design:

Cognitive and analytical value:
The selected visual encodings enhance pattern recognition and analytical understanding. The map supports spatial reasoning, the histogram reveals distributions and outliers, the bar chart enables comparison, and the line chart highlights trends over time. Interactivity further supports exploration and deeper insight.

Communicative value:
The use of simple, well-known chart types combined with a structured narrative ensures that the information is communicated clearly to a broad audience. Annotations and explanations help users with limited data literacy understand the key insights.

Experiential and aesthetic value:
A clean and minimal visual design combined with light interactivity creates an engaging user experience. The ability to explore the data (e.g. through tooltips or zooming) increases user involvement and trust in the visualization, while maintaining clarity and usability.

Justification:
The visualization concept directly supports the project objectives by enabling users to understand meteorite patterns in an intuitive and structured way. It addresses user needs by simplifying complex data, reducing cognitive effort, and providing both guided storytelling and limited interactive exploration.



## Project Plan
Divide the project into individual phases, describe them briefly and draw up a preliminary timetable, e.g. as a Gantt chart:

```{mermaid}
%%| label: fig-project-plan
%%| fig-cap: Planned vs Actual project plan in the form of a Gantt chart.
%%{init: {
  "theme": "base",
  "themeVariables": {
    "taskBkgColor": "#E0E0E0",
    "taskBorderColor": "#9E9E9E",
    "taskTextDarkColor": "#000000",
    "doneTaskBkgColor": "#8A2BE2",
    "doneTaskBorderColor": "#4B0082",
    "activeTaskBkgColor": "#9370DB",
    "activeTaskBorderColor": "#4B0082",
    "activeTaskTextDarkColor": "#FFFFFF",
    "doneTaskTextDarkColor": "#FFFFFF"
  }
}}%%
gantt
    title Project Plan (Planned vs. Actual)
    dateFormat YYYY-MM-DD
    
    section Project Foundation
    Project setup & Charta (Plan)                 :plan_a1, 2026-03-18, 2d
    Project setup & Charta (Actual)               :done, act_a1, 2026-03-18, 2d
    
    Data Sourcing (Plan)                          :plan_a2, 2026-03-18, 2d
    Data Sourcing (Actual)                        :done, act_a2, 2026-03-19, 3d
    
    Coaching & Concept Dev. (Plan)                :plan_a3, 2026-03-25, 3d
    Coaching & Concept Dev. (Actual)              :done, act_a3, 2026-03-25, 2d
    
    Organization & Version Control (Plan)         :plan_a4, 2026-03-28, 2d
    Organization & Version Control (Actual)       :done, act_a4, 2026-03-28, 2d

    section Data Enrichment
    Price Index Research (Plan)                   :plan_a5, 2026-04-14, 2d
    Price Index Research (Actual)                 :active, act_a5, 2026-04-14, 3d
    
    Environmental Data acquisition (Plan)         :plan_a6, 2026-04-16, 1d
    Environmental Data acquisition (Actual)       :active, act_a6, 2026-04-16, 1d
    
    Spatial Join (Climate/Pop) (Plan)             :plan_a7, 2026-04-16, 5d
    Spatial Join (Climate/Pop) (Actual)           :active, act_a7, 2026-04-16, 5d

    section Visualization
    Bivariate Map Development (Plan)              :plan_a8, 2026-04-18, 10d
    Bivariate Map Development (Actual)            :active, act_a8, 2026-04-18, 10d
    
    Valuation UI & Storytelling (Plan)            :plan_a9, 2026-04-18, 7d
    Valuation UI & Storytelling (Actual)          :active, act_a9, 2026-04-18, 7d

    section Finalization
    Documentation & Refinement (Plan)             :plan_a10, 2026-04-29, 10d
    Documentation & Refinement (Actual)           :active, act_a10, 2026-04-29, 10d
    
    Storytelling improvements (Plan)              :plan_a11, 2026-05-02, 5d
    Storytelling improvements (Actual)            :active, act_a11, 2026-05-02, 5d

    Buffer for unforseen issues (Plan)            :plan_a11, 2026-05-10, 7d
    Buffer for unforseen issues (Actual)          :active, act_a11, 2026-05-10, 7d
    
    Final Review & Submission (Plan)              :plan_a12, 2026-05-25, 2d
    Final Review & Submission (Actual)            :active, act_a12, 2026-05-25, 2d
```

See [Mermaid syntax for Gantt charts](https://mermaid.js.org/syntax/gantt.html). It might not be displayed correctly in Safari &#8594; use Chrome. [Live editor with export functionality](https://mermaid.live/)

## Roles and Contact Details
List the people involved in the development work here with their role titles, tasks and contact details.
Team Members

- Baskaran Amutha (baskaamu): Technical Setup and Data Integration
- Eros Palumbo (palero01): Project Lead and Concept & Socio-Economic Analysis.
- Nicolas Schwaiger (schwani8): Design, Visualization & Layout Support.
